originalTitle: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
title: Good News Soon: Our School Friends Are Missing, and We Are Looking for Them!
content:
Hi there, super explorers and bright young minds! It's me, your friendly African news reporter, zooming in from our amazing continent! I'm here to share some news with you, and remember, even when news is a little bit sad, we always look for the good in people and hope for happy endings.

Hello from Africa!

We are talking about a wonderful country called Nigeria today. Nigeria is a very, very big country in Africa, with so many people, beautiful places, and busy towns. Imagine a country that's home to millions of smiling faces, just like yours! Our story takes us to a part of Nigeria called Borno State, and inside Borno State, there's a town named Mussa. *Did you know?* Africa is the second-biggest continent in the whole wide world! And Nigeria is one of its biggest countries, full of different cultures and languages!

What Happened in Mussa Town?

Something not so good happened in Mussa town. Some grown-ups who do bad things went to the town. They took away some of our school friends. These children were at their school or maybe near their homes, just like you might be playing or learning. This made everyone in Mussa town, and many people all over Nigeria and even the world, very, very sad and worried. When something like this makes a whole town feel sad and worried, we say it has "devastated" the town, meaning it made everyone feel very down. It's important to remember that these children did nothing wrong. They were just going about their day, excited to learn and play, just like you. Bad things like this happen sometimes, but it's never the fault of the children.

Who Are These Children?

Our friends who are missing are schoolchildren. Some of them are just little toddlers, which means they are very, very young, maybe even younger than you are! Others are a bit older, like big brothers and sisters. They all love going to school because school is a place where you learn so many amazing things. You learn to read stories, write your name, count numbers, and make new friends. *Did you know?* Going to school helps you grow your brain and learn how to do big, wonderful things when you get older! Every child deserves to go to school and be safe there. Imagine not seeing your friends in class one day. That's how the other children and teachers in Mussa town are feeling right now. They miss their friends and classmates very much, and they want them to come back safely.

Many Grown-Ups Are Helping!

But here's the really important part: many, many grown-ups are working super hard to find our missing school friends! It's like when you lose your favorite toy, and all the grown-ups in your house help you search for it. But this time, it's an even bigger search! Parents, teachers, and neighbours in Mussa town are helping. Also, important people like the police are looking. The police are like special helpers who keep everyone safe and look for people who do bad things. The army, which is made up of brave soldiers, is also helping to search every corner. And even the big leaders of Nigeria, our government, are helping too. They are all working together, using their brains and their strength, to bring these children back home. They want to make sure every child is safe and sound. When something sad happens, it makes us see how much people care about each other and how they come together to help. *Did you know?* Police officers and soldiers are very brave people who work hard every day to protect everyone in their country! They are like superheroes in real life!

Why Do Bad Things Happen?

Sometimes, grown-ups do things that are wrong and hurt others. We don't always know exactly why they do it, but it's never okay. It's important to know that most people in the world are good and kind, and they want to help. These bad people are a very small number, and they don't represent all the wonderful people in Nigeria or anywhere else in Africa. Everyone deserves to feel safe and happy, especially children.

Let's All Hope for Them

So, what can we do? We can all send our kind thoughts and wishes to the children, their families, and all the brave grown-ups who are looking for them. We can hope with all our hearts that our school friends will be found very, very soon and brought back to their homes and schools. When a community works together, even through tough times, they show how strong and caring they are. Always remember to listen to your grown-ups and stay safe. The world is a wonderful place, and most people are kind and good. Let's keep hoping for good news from Mussa town. We believe in the power of people helping people, and we know our friends will be back soon! Thank you for listening, my wonderful young friends! *Did you know?* When people help each other, especially when things are tough, it makes everyone feel stronger and happier! We are all one big family on this planet!
summary: Some young schoolchildren in Mussa town, Nigeria, are missing, and many grown-ups like parents, police, and the army are working very hard to find them and bring them home safely.
whyMatters: This is important because every child deserves to be safe, go to school, and be with their family, and we want to make sure everyone knows that people are working hard to help our missing friends.

originalTitle: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
title: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
content: President Donald Trump said the operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

originalTitle: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo
title: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo
content: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported - a case has also been reported in Uganda.

originalTitle: DRC health minister warns ‘very high’ Ebola lethality rate as toll hits 80
title: DRC health minister warns ‘very high’ Ebola lethality rate as toll hits 80
content: Africa CDC concerned that outbreak of Bundibugyo strain could spread rapidly due to intense population movement.

originalTitle: Thousands march in central London to mark 78th Nakba anniversary
title: Thousands march in central London to mark 78th Nakba anniversary
content: Thousands of protesters marched through central London on Saturday to mark the 78th anniversary of the Nakba

originalTitle: Palestinian President Abbas casts vote in decisive Fatah elections
title: Palestinian President Abbas casts vote in decisive Fatah elections
content: Internal Fatah leadership elections take place amid questions of who will eventually succeed the 90-year-old leader.

originalTitle: Taiwan insists it is independent after Trump warning
title: Taiwan insists it is independent after Trump warning
content: Fresh from his summit in Beijing, the US president warned against a formal declaration of independence by the island.

originalTitle: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
title: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
content: President Donald Trump said the operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

originalTitle: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
title: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
content: Staff Sgt Mohamed Mahdhee died while diving in the search-and-rescue operation, the Maldives government tells the BBC.

originalTitle: 'Menacing and threatening atmosphere' forced Hearts exit
title: 'Menacing and threatening atmosphere' forced Hearts exit
content: Hearts say "the menacing and threatening atmosphere inside the stadium" forced them to leave Celtic Park immediately after the final whistle of their Scottish Premiership decider.

originalTitle: Chelsea closing in on appointing Alonso as next manager
title: Chelsea closing in on appointing Alonso as next manager
content: Chelsea are closing in on appointing Spaniard Xabi Alonso as the club's next permanent head coach.

originalTitle: Resilient Celtic time run perfectly to win race after eight-month chase
title: Resilient Celtic time run perfectly to win race after eight-month chase
content: Celtic chased Hearts for eight months, 32 games, 2,880 on-field minutes and 48 hours. In the season's dying embers, they caught them.
